functional.text: Score calculation uses a weighted formula: score = Σ(factor_weight × factor_value) where factors include demographic fit, behavioral signals, and engagement recency
Leads are classified into three tiers based on configurable thresholds: Hot (>= hot_threshold), Warm (>= warm_threshold and < hot_threshold), Cold (< warm_threshold)
Scoring weights and thresholds are stored in a configuration table, editable via an admin API without deployment
Scores recalculate when any input factor changes (CRM field update, form submission, email engagement event)
Score changes that cross a tier boundary trigger an in-app notification to the assigned sales rep
The scoring report endpoint returns lead distribution by tier and conversion rates for a given date range
All score calculations are logged with input factors, weights used, and final score for auditability
When a new lead is created, the system calls the Clearbit Enrichment API to fetch firmographic data (company size, industry, employee count) and stores it as scoring factors

non_functional.text: Score calculation P95 latency < 200ms per lead
Batch re-scoring (when weights change) must complete within 5 minutes for up to 10,000 active leads
Score history retained for 12 months for audit and accuracy analysis
Configuration changes are audit-logged with who changed what and when
The scoring endpoint must handle 50 concurrent scoring requests without degradation
Enrichment API calls must respect the external rate limit (600 requests/min). Use a queue with rate limiting to prevent 429 responses

edge_cases.scenario: Lead has no optional factors filled
edge_cases.behavior: Return score of 0, tier cold. Do not fail — log warning for sales ops team

edge_cases.scenario: Scoring weights changed while batch re-score is in progress
edge_cases.behavior: Queue new re-score job after current one completes. Do not cancel in-progress job

edge_cases.scenario: Two CRM events arrive for same lead within 1 second
edge_cases.behavior: Deduplicate by lead_id with 2-second debounce window. Only most recent event triggers scoring
Fall back to PostgreSQL direct reads. Dashboard slower (200ms → ~500ms) but functional. Log cache miss

edge_cases.scenario: Config update sets hot_threshold lower than warm_threshold
edge_cases.behavior: Reject with 400 error: Hot threshold must be greater than warm threshold

edge_cases.scenario: Score calculation produces value outside 0-100 range
edge_cases.behavior: Clamp to 0-100. Log alert — indicates weight configuration error

edge_cases.scenario: Clearbit Enrichment API is unavailable or returns an error
edge_cases.behavior: Score the lead with available data only. Do not block lead creation. Queue a retry for enrichment. Log the failure

fields.name: source
fields.description: How the lead arrived (organic, paid, referral)

fields.name: assigned_rep_id
fields.description: Sales rep assigned to this lead

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the lead entered the system

fields.name: factors
fields.description: Snapshot of input factors and weights used

api_contracts.purpose: Trigger score calculation for a specific lead

errors.meaning: Lead has insufficient data for scoring

api_contracts.purpose: Retrieve current scoring weights and thresholds
Update scoring weights and/or thresholds. Triggers async re-scoring.

errors.meaning: Invalid weight (negative or > 100) or invalid threshold
User does not have scoring admin permissions

api_contracts.purpose: Lead distribution and conversion rates by tier for a date range

clarifications.question: Where does budget_declared come from? Is it a CRM field, a form input, or derived?
clarifications.answer: It's a field in the lead intake form. Free-text number field, stored as integer in the CRM.

clarifications.question: FR-005 says trigger a notification when a tier boundary is crossed. What channel?
clarifications.answer: In-app notification for v1. Slack integration later as a separate feature.

clarifications.question: What happens if two sales ops managers update scoring config simultaneously?
clarifications.answer: Last write wins. No optimistic locking for v1 — only 2 people with config access.
